title: Convolutional Projection
concept: Using depthwise convolutions to project keys, queries, and values in self-attention blocks adds local inductive bias and reduces parameter count.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is a key structural difference between LeViT and standard ViT-Base?

options.text: LeViT uses Batch Normalization and relative attention biases instead of LayerNorm and absolute embeddings.

exercises.explain: Correct! LeViT optimizes for edge speed by utilizing BatchNorm and relative biases.

cards.title: Convolutions inside attention
cards.body: So far, we've looked at putting convolutions *before* attention. But what if we put them *inside* the attention block itself? By replacing standard linear projections with depthwise convolutions, we can inject local context into the query, key, and value vectors. Let's see how.

cards.title: Convolutional Projection
cards.body: In convolutional projection, the Query (Q), Key (K), and Value (V) vectors are generated using depthwise separable convolutions instead of simple linear layers. This enforces local spatial correlation within the attention mechanism itself, adding a local prior.

cards.title: A smart reading glasses magnifying lens
cards.body: A standard projection is like looking at a word in isolation. A convolutional projection is like using a magnifying glass that lets you see the word *and* its adjacent neighbors simultaneously before you write down a summary. It adds local context to every query.

cards.title: CvT (Convolutional Transformer)
cards.body: The CvT architecture replaces the standard linear projections of Q, K, and V in the self-attention block with depthwise convolutions. This allows the model to achieve top performance on ImageNet without needing position embeddings!

cards.title: No parameters explosion
cards.body: You might worry that adding convolutions inside attention will make the model massive. Because we use depthwise convolutions (which process each channel individually), the parameter count and computational overhead are kept extremely low.

left.text:
1x1 projection matrix
Processes each token in isolation
Requires position embeddings

right.text:
Depthwise convolution matrix
Processes token + spatial neighbors
Bypasses absolute position embeddings

exercises.prompt: How does convolutional projection generate Q, K, and V vectors inside attention blocks?
exercises.explain: Depthwise convolutions replace standard linear projection matrices, injecting local spatial priors into Q, K, and V.

options.text: By using depthwise convolutions instead of standard linear layers
By multiplying vectors with random noise matrices
By running recurrent loops over the channel dimensions
By deleting the Query vectors entirely

exercises.prompt: True or false: Models like CvT that use convolutional projections can perform well without absolute position embeddings.
exercises.explain: True. The convolutional projection inherently injects spatial coordinate order and locality, making absolute position embeddings optional.
Depthwise convolutions ensure that each token embedding aggregates information from its spatial neighbors.
exercises.sentence: By replacing linear layers with depthwise convolutions, we add spatial ___ directly to the query, key, and value matrices.

exercises.prompt: Which of these benefits is achieved by using depthwise convolutions in convolutional projections?
exercises.explain: Depthwise convolutions process channels independently, providing local feature extraction with minimal parameters.

options.text: Adding local spatial context while keeping parameter overhead extremely low
Eliminating the need for loss calculation
Increasing the number of attention heads to infinity

exercises.prompt: Why does convolutional projection make a transformer more robust to changes in input image resolution?

options.text: Because the convolution filters automatically adapt to different stride steps and sizes, and the model doesn't rely on fixed-length absolute position embedding grids.
Because it forces the image to be square at all times.
Because it converts all color channels to grayscale.
Because it doubles the size of the attention matrices.

exercises.explain: Brilliant! Convolutions are translation-invariant and generalize naturally to different shapes, bypassing the rigid grid constraints of absolute position embeddings. Next: let's explore one of the most popular modern vision backbones — the Swin Transformer!
